text:
In a small village by the sea lived a cat named Felix. Felix was an adventurous little pussycat. Every morning he’d sneak out from his sunny bed and meander along the cobblestone pathways beside the old oak tree. One day, while lazily propping himself onto a stone stump, Felix heard something strange. It sounded like whispers in the wind.

Curiosity getting the better of him, Felix followed the humming sounds up into the branches of the towering oak. Among the rusty hooks and gnarled branches he stumbled upon a craggy treasure chest. Opening it seemed almost magical—they fluttered out delicate feathers! Felix pocketed them and scampered down.

Later that evening, Felix dreamed of treasure islands with beaches of pearls. He dreamt he could sail back home. The next day, he practiced sailing tricks on his own leash. His adventures ended when he accidentally tumbled into owl territory—dark and solemn—then he was caught. An owl's stare felt icy.
